description_long: This is a cylindrical spray can with a visibly weathered and rusted metal exterior, featuring a mottled surface of reddish-brown and blueish hues. The can tapers slightly at the top, where a stepped, circular plastic nozzle assembly is mounted, capped by a small, round actuator button with a red dot at its center. The surface of the can is heavily textured, showing signs of corrosion and pitting, giving it an aged, neglected appearance. The nozzle is off-white and appears to be made of plastic, contrasting with the darker, metallic body. There are no visible labels, logos, or text on the can, and the overall finish is matte and rough. The object is typical of an urban environment, possibly discarded or left unused for a long period.
description: A rusted, weathered spray can with a rough, mottled metal body and a stepped plastic nozzle. The can shows heavy corrosion and lacks any visible branding or labels.
description_view_0: Front view shows the spray can's actuator button with a red dot and the most prominent rust and pitting on the metal body.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the stepped plastic nozzle and the uneven, corroded texture of the can's surface.
description_view_2: Another side view, showing the can's cylindrical shape and extensive rust, with the nozzle's actuator less visible.
description_view_3: Rear view displays the continuous rusted surface and the full profile of the stepped nozzle, with no actuator button visible.
attribute_damage_or_deformation: Heavy rust, corrosion, and surface pitting visible on the metal body
